content:
# MSFT Quarterly Earnings Summary (FY24 Q2 – FY25 Q2)

## Revenue & EPS vs Estimates

| Quarter | Revenue | YoY | vs Est | EPS | YoY | vs Est |
|---------|---------|-----|--------|-----|-----|--------|
| FY25 Q2 | $69.63B | +12% | ✅ +$1.0B | $3.23 | +10% | ✅ +$0.12 |
| FY25 Q1 | $65.59B | +16% | ✅ +$1.1B | $3.30 | +10% | ✅ +$0.20 |
| FY24 Q4 | $64.73B | +15% | ❌ Miss | $2.95 | +10% | ✅ +$0.02 |
| FY24 Q3 | $61.86B | +17% | ✅ +$0.5B | $2.94 | +20% | ✅ +$0.12 |
| FY24 Q2 | $62.02B | +18% | ✅ +$1.3B | $2.93 | +33% | ✅ +$0.15 |

## Growth Rates

| Quarter | Revenue QoQ | Revenue YoY | Net Income | NI YoY |
|---------|-------------|-------------|------------|--------|
| FY25 Q2 | +6.2% | +12% | $24.11B | +10% |
| FY25 Q1 | +1.3% | +16% | $24.67B | +11% |
| FY24 Q4 | +4.6% | +15% | $22.04B | +10% |
| FY24 Q3 | -0.3% | +17% | $21.94B | +20% |
| FY24 Q2 | +9.7% | +18% | $21.87B | +33% |

## Margins

| Quarter | Gross | Operating | Net |
|---------|-------|-----------|-----|
| FY25 Q2 | 68.7% | 45.5% | 34.6% |
| FY25 Q1 | 69.4% | 46.6% | 37.6% |
| FY24 Q4 | 69.6% | 43.1% | 34.0% |
| FY24 Q3 | 70.1% | 44.6% | 35.5% |
| FY24 Q2 | 68.4% | 43.6% | 35.3% |

## FY25 Q3 Guidance

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Revenue | $67.7B–$68.7B (~13% YoY) |
| Azure Growth | 31-32% CC |
| OpEx | $16.0B–$16.1B |
| CapEx | ~$22B |
| AI Run Rate | $13B annual (+175% YoY) |

## One-Time Items

| Quarter | Item | Impact |
|---------|------|--------|
| FY25 Q2 | Investment losses | -$2.3B |
| FY25 Q1 | Activision lapping | Xbox +2% vs +61% prior |
| FY24 Q2 | Activision acquisition | +55-61 pts Xbox growth |
| FY23 Q2 | Severance/impairment | -$1.2B charge |

## Key Metrics

- **Beat Rate:** 5/6 quarters (revenue estimates)
- **YoY Revenue Growth Range:** 12%–18%
- **YoY EPS Growth Range:** 10%–33%
- **Operating Margin Expansion:** 43.6% → 45.5%
- **Azure AI Contribution:** 12-13 pts of Azure growth
- **Annual CapEx Plan:** ~$80B+
rawContent:
Now I have all the data needed. Let me compile the comprehensive analysis:

---

# Microsoft (MSFT) Quarterly Earnings Analysis
### Last 6 Reported Quarters (FY24 Q2 – FY25 Q2)

---

## 1. Revenue Per Quarter — Actual vs Estimates

| Quarter | Period Ended | Revenue (Actual) | YoY Growth | Beat/Miss |
|---------|--------------|------------------|------------|-----------|
| **FY25 Q2** | Dec 31, 2024 | **$69.63B** | +12% | ✅ Beat (~$1B above $68.5B est) |
| **FY25 Q1** | Sep 30, 2024 | **$65.59B** | +16% | ✅ Beat (~$1.1B above $64.5B est) |
| **FY24 Q4** | Jun 30, 2024 | **$64.73B** | +15% | ❌ Slight miss (est ~$64.4B, but Azure disappointed) |
| **FY24 Q3** | Mar 31, 2024 | **$61.86B** | +17% | ✅ Beat (~$500M above $61.1B est) |
| **FY24 Q2** | Dec 31, 2023 | **$62.02B** | +18% | ✅ Beat (~$1.3B above $61.1B est) |

---

## 2. EPS Per Quarter — Actual vs Estimates

| Quarter | EPS (Diluted) | YoY Growth | Beat/Miss |
|---------|---------------|------------|-----------|
| **FY25 Q2** | **$3.23** | +10% | ✅ Beat (est ~$3.11) |
| **FY25 Q1** | **$3.30** | +10% | ✅ Beat (est ~$3.10) |
| **FY24 Q4** | **$2.95** | +10% | ✅ Beat (est ~$2.93) |
| **FY24 Q3** | **$2.94** | +20% | ✅ Beat (est ~$2.82) |
| **FY24 Q2** | **$2.93** | +33% | ✅ Beat (est ~$2.78) |

---

## 3. Revenue Growth Rates

| Quarter | Revenue | QoQ Growth | YoY Growth |
|---------|---------|------------|------------|
| **FY25 Q2** | $69.63B | +6.2% | +12% |
| **FY25 Q1** | $65.59B | +1.3% | +16% |
| **FY24 Q4** | $64.73B | +4.6% | +15% |
| **FY24 Q3** | $61.86B | -0.3% | +17% |
| **FY24 Q2** | $62.02B | +9.7% | +18% |

**Trend:** YoY growth decelerated from 18% to 12% as comps became tougher post-Activision integration.

---

## 4. Earnings Growth Rates

| Quarter | Net Income | QoQ Growth | YoY Growth |
|---------|------------|------------|------------|
| **FY25 Q2** | $24.11B | -2.3% | +10% |
| **FY25 Q1** | $24.67B | +11.9% | +11% |
| **FY24 Q4** | $22.04B | +0.5% | +10% |
| **FY24 Q3** | $21.94B | +0.3% | +20% |
| **FY24 Q2** | $21.87B | — | +33% |

**Trend:** YoY EPS growth normalized from 33% to 10% as FY23 Q2 had one-time charges ($1.2B severance/impairment).

---

## 5. Margin Trends

| Quarter | Gross Margin | Operating Margin | Net Margin |
|---------|--------------|------------------|------------|
| **FY25 Q2** | **68.7%** | **45.5%** | **34.6%** |
| **FY25 Q1** | **69.4%** | **46.6%** | **37.6%** |
| **FY24 Q4** | **69.6%** | **43.1%** | **34.0%** |
| **FY24 Q3** | **70.1%** | **44.6%** | **35.5%** |
| **FY24 Q2** | **68.4%** | **43.6%** | **35.3%** |

**Key Observations:**
- Gross margins remain healthy at ~69%, slight compression in FY25 Q2 due to AI infrastructure costs
- Operating margin expanded from 43.6% to 45.5% (FY24 Q2 → FY25 Q2)
- Net margin pressure in FY25 Q2 from "Other expense" of $2.3B (investment losses)

---

## 6. Forward Guidance (FY25 Q2 Call - Jan 2025)

**FY25 Q3 Guidance:**
| Metric | Guidance |
|--------|----------|
| **Total Revenue** | $67.7B–$68.7B (implies ~13% YoY) |
| **Azure Growth** | ~31-32% in constant currency |
| **OpEx** | $16.0B–$16.1B |
| **CapEx** | ~$22B for Q3 (AI infrastructure heavy) |

**Full Year FY25 Expectations:**
- Azure growth to accelerate in H2 FY25 as AI capacity comes online
- AI revenue run rate now **$13B annually** (up 175% YoY)
- Microsoft Cloud targeting ~$42B quarterly run rate

---

## 7. Notable One-Time Items & Adjustments

| Quarter | Item | Impact |
|---------|------|--------|
| **FY25 Q2** | Investment losses (equity method) | -$2.3B in "Other expense" |
| **FY25 Q1** | Activision lapping | Xbox revenue normalized (+2% vs +61% prior quarters) |
| **FY24 Q4** | FY23 restructuring comparison | Non-GAAP adjustments removed |
| **FY24 Q2** | Activision acquisition (Oct 2023) | +55-61 pts to Xbox growth |
| **FY23 Q2** | Severance + Hardware impairment | $1.2B charge (creates easy comps) |

---

## Key Takeaways

1. **Consistent beats** — MSFT has beaten revenue estimates 5 of last 6 quarters
2. **AI monetization accelerating** — $13B AI run rate, up from $5B a year ago
3. **Azure growth stable** — 29-33% range, with AI contributing 12-13 points
4. **Margin discipline** — Operating margins expanding despite heavy CapEx
5. **CapEx surge** — ~$80B+ annual infrastructure spend planned for AI buildout
6. **Watch item:** Net margin compression from investment portfolio volatility
